message:
fix(func): use Kahan compensated summation in aggregate functions; expand corruption injector test coverage

agg_builtins.rs: Introduces Kahan compensated summation to the avg(),
sum(), and total() aggregate functions. C SQLite's aggregate accumulator
uses an internal compensation technique to preserve low-order bits lost
during floating-point addition. Without this, FrankenSQLite's naive
summation would silently diverge from C SQLite on workloads with many
small values or mixed-magnitude additions (a known source of correctness
test failures in the E2E suite). The fix adds a kahan_add() helper that
maintains a running compensation term, and threads a compensation field
through AvgState, SumState, and the new TotalState struct (replacing the
bare f64 that total() previously used). Test assertions for float
comparisons are also tightened to use assert!(matches!(...)) instead of
panic!() for better diagnostics on failure.

corruption.rs: Adds 19 new tests to the CorruptionInjector test suite,
providing thorough coverage of edge cases and error paths:

  BitFlip: all 8 bit positions, surrounding byte preservation,
  double-flip idempotency (XOR self-inverse), file boundary injection,
  and rejection of invalid bit_position (>=8) and out-of-bounds offsets.

  PageZero: first-page zeroing, last-page zeroing, and out-of-range
  page number rejection.

  RandomOverwrite: different seeds produce different bytes,
  out-of-range offset+length rejection, and affected_pages spanning
  across page boundaries.

  PagePartialCorrupt: page-boundary-aligned corruption, deterministic
  reproduction from seed, different-seed divergence, cross-page-boundary
  rejection, and offset-out-of-range rejection.

  HeaderZero: verifies SQLite magic bytes are destroyed, and that the
  resulting database fails rusqlite's integrity_check.

  WalFrameCorrupt: single-frame-only corruption (other frames intact),
  deterministic reproduction, and out-of-range frame rejection.

  inject_many: validates sequential application of multiple patterns.

  report: verifies original_sha256 matches expected pre-corruption hash.

Also adds a temp_db_filled() helper for creating test databases with
specific fill bytes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
